title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight text format for data exchange. It's a string, not a JS object — but it looks very similar.

Key operations:
• JSON.stringify(obj, replacer, space) — converts a JS value to a JSON string.
• JSON.parse(str, reviver) — converts a JSON string back to a JS value.

JSON rules (stricter than JS):
• Keys must be double-quoted strings.
• No comments, no trailing commas, no undefined, no functions.
• Values: string, number, boolean, null, array, object.

Deep clone trick: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj)) — quick but loses functions, dates, undefined, and circular references.
